name: Command Injection in ping form
description: User-supplied 'ip' parameter is concatenated into a shell command and passed to shell_exec() without sanitization, enabling arbitrary OS command execution as the web server user.
fix_strategy: Validate 'ip' against a strict IPv4/IPv6 regex; pass through escapeshellarg() before concatenation; prefer running the ping via proc_open with an argv array (no shell). Reference patch is in DVWA impossible.php.

name: SQL Injection in user lookup
description: The 'id' GET parameter is interpolated directly into a SELECT statement, allowing UNION-based extraction of arbitrary tables including the users table (which stores password hashes).
fix_strategy: Replace mysqli_query with a PDO prepared statement; bind 'id' as an integer parameter; ensure error messages are not surfaced to the client. Reference patch is in DVWA impossible.php.

name: Unrestricted File Upload
description: The upload form accepts any file extension and content type, writing the result to a web-accessible directory served by PHP. An attacker can upload a .php webshell and execute arbitrary code.
fix_strategy: Validate uploaded file by magic bytes (not extension); rename to a UUID with a fixed safe extension on disk; store outside the web root or in a directory with PHP execution disabled; chmod 0644. Reference patch is in DVWA impossible.php.

name: Stored XSS in guestbook
description: User-supplied name and message fields are saved to the database and rendered to other users without HTML encoding, allowing persistent JavaScript injection that fires for every viewer of the guestbook page.
fix_strategy: Apply htmlspecialchars($value, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') on output; add a strict Content-Security-Policy header (script-src 'self'); set HttpOnly and SameSite on the session cookie. Reference patch is in DVWA impossible.php.
